page_summary: A page featuring three paragraphs of Old Turkic (GГ¶ktГјrk) runic text above two circular illustrations: a geometric cross-and-dots design on the left and a pictorial scene with a tree, figures and architecture on the right.

symbols.description: Row of Old Turkic runic glyphs (GГ¶ktГјrk script), black ink
Small symbol/glyph at the top center of the left circle, resembling a stylized 'T' or anchor with a dot above it
symbols.position: top center inside the left circle

symbols.description: Small round dots / circles distributed in the quadrants of the left circle
symbols.position: inside the left circle, four quadrants

decorations.description: A large circle divided into four quadrants by a vertical and horizontal cross/line, with a small symbol at top center and small open circles distributed in each quadrant (approximately three circles per quadrant, suggesting a 3x3-like arrangement of dots within a quartered disc).
components: small anchor/T-like symbol at top center
small open circles scattered in the four quadrants

decorations.description: A large circle containing a pictorial scene: a central tree/column-like form with a sun or halo at its top, two small human figures standing on the left side of a horizontal structure (possibly a bridge or building with railings/posts), a smaller figure near the base of the tree, two circular emblems (one with a cross/quarter pattern on the left, one similar on the right), and a small rectangular base at the bottom.
components: central tree/trunk with sun-disc at top
two small human figures on the left
one human figure at the base of the tree
two circular medallions (left and right) with cross/quartered patterns

uncertain_or_unreadable: The three text blocks are written in Old Turkic / GГ¶ktГјrk runic script; the glyphs are difficult to transcribe with certainty, so the provided transcription is an approximation and may contain errors.
The exact meaning of the small symbol at the top of the left circle is unclear.
The number of small circles in each quadrant of the left circle is hard to count exactly.
The right circle's scene is highly stylized; the identity of the figures, the structure, and the medallions cannot be determined with certainty from the image alone.
